mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll expansion raises expected household income and corporate revenues; when PAYEMS trends upward over three months, the S&P 500 earns positive forward 20-day returns as earnings expectations are revised higher.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 135046 thousand as of the February 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has nonpositive average 20-day forward S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 in more than two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned below 0.25% lowers discount rates and supports equity duration; when FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% and remains there, the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% and remains below 0.25% for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.15 as of the February 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not above unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal stronger final demand and near-term revenue growth; when RSAFS accelerates over three months, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns through upward sales and margin expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 421399 million dollars as of the February 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if its walk-forward directional hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in housing starts above recovery thresholds signals stronger construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet repair, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 800 thousand annualized units after being below 800 thousand within the prior 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 890 thousand units as of the January 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above 800 are followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness combined with a high unemployment rate identifies a growth scare; when INDPRO falls while UNRATE remains above 7%, earnings risk dominates policy support and S&P 500 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is negative and UNRATE is above 7.0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.7 as of the February 2013 snapshot
regime_hint: fragile recovery with elevated labor slack
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if interaction terms add no out-of-sample predictive power versus INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration with no offsetting rise in FEDFUNDS compresses real purchasing power and profit margins while increasing future tightening risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.50%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 232.77 as of the February 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings; when GDPC1 rises versus the prior quarter, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns as recession probability declines.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 13656.8 billions of chained 2017 dollars as of the lagged quarterly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with positive GDPC1 growth have lower subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than quarters with nonpositive GDPC1 growth in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in unemployment below 8% after a high-slack period signals labor-market normalization without immediate overheating, improving confidence and expected revenues, so S&P 500 20-day returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 8.0% after being above 8.0% within the prior 6 months and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.7 as of the February 2013 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if this joint labor trigger has a 20-day S&P 500 hit rate below 50% or if triggered mean returns are not statistically above unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.
